evals.name: positive-trigger-full-sweep
evals.prompt: Give me today's psychiatry paper digest across all the subspecialties.
evals.expected_output: Triggers. Loads .psych-paper-digest.json, windows each domain from its last_swept minus 3 days, sweeps PubMed at the evidence floor and ClinicalTrials.gov for the three readout events, dedups against the newest previous digest file, and writes digest-YYYY-MM-DD.md with the header count line and the four sections Act / Read / Registry watch / Suppressed. Every item carries a number and a DOI link only (no authors, journal, year, or PMID in the body). Chat close is two lines with domains/kept/screened/trials counts and the Act count. last_swept advances on every swept domain only after the file is written.

evals.name: negative-trigger-decision-question
evals.prompt: Should I start lisdexamfetamine for this 9-year-old with ADHD who failed methylphenidate?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger. This is a treatment decision for a patient in front of the user — routes to pubmed-research-note. No sweep, no digest, no watchlist read.

evals.name: negative-trigger-single-topic-research
evals.prompt: What does the literature say about ketamine for treatment-resistant depression?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger. One named topic with a decision implied is pubmed-research-note's entry point, not a watchlist sweep. Defers there.

evals.name: first-run-init
evals.expected_output: No .psych-paper-digest.json exists. Runs init: elicits 3-8 watchlist domains, offering vault MOC titles as candidates if vault/MOCs/ exists at the marketplace root; drafts each domain's PubMed query and trials_term and shows them for one confirm pass; writes the config with every last_swept: null; then runs the inaugural sweep with a 14-day window in the same turn. Does not sweep before the config is confirmed and written.

evals.name: catch-up-window-capped
evals.prompt: Paper digest please — I haven't run this since February.
evals.expected_output: last_swept is more than 90 days old. Sweeps a 90-day window, names the cap and the uncovered gap in the digest header line rather than silently absorbing it. last_swept advances only after the digest file is written; a failed run would leave it untouched.

evals.name: triage-inflation-guard
evals.expected_output: The window holds only small confirmatory positive trials and below-floor items. Act is EMPTY and carries the explicit single line 'Nothing practice-changing this window — all clear.' — not padded, not apologized for. Confirmatory positives sit in Read with their numbers; the rest are counted per domain under Suppressed, never listed. FAILS if a confirmatory me-too result or anything observational without regulator-grade harm data appears in Act.

evals.name: act-item-shape-and-handoff
evals.prompt: What's new in the literature since last sweep?
evals.expected_output: The window contains an adequately powered reversal RCT in a watchlist domain. The Act item carries: the one number that makes it practice-changing (effect size / n / event rate), the decision it raises phrased as a question, the handoff line to pubmed-research-note, and a DOI link. FAILS if the item also answers the question — any verdict, dose recommendation, or adjudication between trials in the digest is a failure. When the user takes the handoff, the raised decision is passed to pubmed-research-note as the seed.

evals.name: registry-readout-section
evals.expected_output: ClinicalTrials.gov is swept for exactly three events per domain: results newly posted, newly completed with nothing posted, and newly terminated for futility or safety (slow accrual ignored as noise). Qualifying trials appear under '## Registry watch' as 'NCT NNNNNNNN — event, n=NNN, date, domain'. A pivotal results-posting or a standard-of-care futility termination may additionally justify an Act item. If the registry is unreachable, the sweep degrades: PubMed still runs and the registry gap is named in the digest header.

evals.name: pubmed-down-is-fatal
evals.expected_output: PubMed is unreachable via both the bundled MCP server and the E-utilities fallback. The skill states this and STOPS. It does not produce a registry-only digest (which would misrepresent the window as quiet), does not advance any last_swept, and does not fabricate a file.

evals.name: vault-gate-delegation
evals.prompt: Run the digest and vault it.
evals.expected_output: The digest file lands per default (working directory or digest_dir), and because the user explicitly asked, the digest is ALSO handed to vault-keeper as an artifact — passing title, body, target type artifact, and a suggested MOC topic. This skill never resolves a vault path, never writes into vault/, never authors vault frontmatter; vault-keeper reports where it landed. Without the explicit request, no vault handoff happens at all.

evals.name: scoped-domain-run
evals.expected_output: Sweeps ONLY the child-adhd domain. Other domains are not queried and their last_swept values are byte-for-byte untouched. The digest header names the single domain; suppressed counts cover only it. child-adhd's last_swept advances after the file is written.

evals.name: thai-trigger-and-watchlist-management
evals.prompt: มีเปเปอร์ใหม่ไหมอาทิตย์นี้ แล้วช่วยเพิ่ม perinatal depression เข้า watchlist ให้ด้วย
evals.expected_output: Triggers on Thai. Two jobs in one turn: adds a perinatal-depression domain to .psych-paper-digest.json (drafting query + trials_term, confirming once, last_swept: null) and runs the sweep. The new domain sweeps with the 14-day inaugural window; existing domains use their own last_swept. Chat close may be Thai-English code-switch; the digest file itself is English.
